steps.title: Fastest path: mark the relationships first
steps.content: Use the given angle relationships first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Establish similarity and find the scale factor
steps.content: Since $\overline{MN} \parallel \overline{UW}$, triangle $VMN$ is similar to triangle $VUW$ (they share angle $V$, and parallel lines create equal corresponding angles). The ratio of the corresponding parallel sides is: $$\frac{MN}{UW} = \frac{24}{72} = \frac{1}{3}$$

steps.title: Apply the similarity ratio to express NW in terms of VW
steps.content: In similar triangles, all corresponding linear measurements scale by the same ratio. Therefore: $$\frac{VN}{VW} = \frac{MN}{UW} = \frac{1}{3}$$ This gives $VN = \frac{1}{3}VW$, so: $$NW = VW - VN = VW - \frac{1}{3}VW = \frac{2}{3}VW$$

steps.title: Find VW using the area formula
steps.content: Since angle $W$ is a right angle, sides $\overline{UW}$ and $\overline{VW}$ are perpendicular, making them the base and height. Substituting the given values into the area formula: $$\frac{1}{2} \cdot 72 \cdot VW = 360$$ $$VW = \frac{720}{72} = 10$$ Therefore, $$NW=\frac{2}{3}\cdot 10=\frac{20}{3}$$
